channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A look inside the most expensive house in the world — the home of the UK’s monarch
item.description: Buckingham Palace in London is worth about $4.9 billion and has about 775 rooms, including royal and guest rooms, offices and bathrooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO and Ukraine to hold emergency talks after Russia's attack with new hypersonic missile
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — NATO and Ukraine will hold emergency talks Tuesday after Russia attacked a central city with an experimental, hypersonic ballistic missile that escalated the nearly 33-month-old war.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and VOLODYMYR YURCHUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Bahamas will refinance part of its debt to protect its famous turquoise waters
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — The Bahamas announced Friday that it will refinance $300 million of its external debt to free up more than $120 million for marine conservation projects and climate change mitigation.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pakistani city mourns 42 Shiite Muslims who were ambushed and killed in a gun attack
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Protesters in Pakistan's restive northwest chanted anti-government slogans and tensions flared Friday after funeral prayers were held for 42 Shiite Muslims who were ambushed and killed by gunmen a day earlier in one of the region's deadliest such assaults in recent years.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police blow up suspicious package outside US Embassy in London
item.description: Law enforcement conducted a “controlled explosion” of a suspicious package outside of the U.S. Embassy in London on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A Norwegian student has been arrested on charges of spying on the US for Russia
item.description: Authorities in Norway say a Norwegian student in his 20s has been arrested on suspicion of spying for Russia and Iran while working as a guard at the U.S. Embassy in Oslo.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawsuit against UK security agency by survivors of Ariana Grande concert bombing is rejected
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A legal claim against Britain’s domestic intelligence agency by more than 300 survivors of a bombing that killed 22 people at a 2017 Ariana Grande concert in Manchester was rejected Friday by a special tribunal. Judges on the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel keeping its ‘eyes open’ for Iranian attacks during Trump transition period, ambassador says
item.description: Israel's Ambassador to the United Nations Danny Danon speaks to Fox News Digital about the threat from Iran during the Trump transition period.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German ex-leader Merkel says she felt sorrow at Trump's comeback and recalls awkward non-handshake
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel says she felt “sorrow” at Donald Trump's return to power and recalls that every meeting with him was “a competition: you or me.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hungary's Orbán vows to disregard international arrest warrant for Netanyahu
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán on Friday denounced the International Criminal Court’s issuing of an arrest warrant for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, saying he would defy it by inviting him to Hungary.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Second Australian teen dies in tainted alcohol case in Laos that has killed 6 tourists
item.description: A second Australian teenager who fell critically ill after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos has died, according to a family statement sent to Australian media.
item.dc:creator: Jintamas Saksornchai And David Rising, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korean leader says past diplomacy only confirmed US hostility
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said his past negotiations with the United States only confirmed Washington’s “unchangeable” hostility toward his country and described his nuclear buildup as the only way to counter external threats, state media said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gunmen fire on vehicles carrying Shiites in northwest Pakistan, killing at least 42
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Gunmen opened fire on vehicles carrying Shiite Muslims in Pakistan's restive northwest on Thursday, killing at least 42 people, including six women, and wounding 20 others in one of the region's deadliest such attacks in recent years, police said.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN nuclear agency’s board condemns Iran for the 2nd time this year for failing to fully cooperate
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The U.N. nuclear watchdog’s board on Thursday condemned Iran for failing to cooperate fully with the agency, the second time it has done so in just five months.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Zealand police begin arrests for gang symbol ban as new law takes effect
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — A ban on New Zealanders wearing or displaying symbols of gang affiliation in public took effect on Thursday, with police officers making their first arrest for a breach of the law three minutes later.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Warrants put Israeli PM and others in a small group of leaders accused of crimes against humanity
item.description: The decision by the International Criminal Court to issue arrest warrants for the Israeli prime minister and a top Hamas official puts them in a small group of leaders to be accused of crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s former President Bolsonaro and aides indicted for alleged 2022 coup attempt
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro and 36 others, including aides, were indicted by federal police on Thursday for allegedly attempting a coup to keep him in office in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Putin says Russia fired intermediate-range ballistic missile at Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday that Russia fired a new, nonnuclear intermediate-range ballistic missile at Ukraine, part of a wide-ranging TV address that sharply criticized the U.S. for escalating the war.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Treasury imposes sanctions on Russian banks, financial officials
item.description: Russia’s third-largest bank, Gazprombank, and its subsidiaries were hit with U.S. sanctions Thursday, making it the largest bank not already targeted as the Russia-Ukraine war nears the three-year mark.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Police in Finland arrest five suspects over separatist violence in southeast Nigeria
item.description: HELSINKI, Finland (AP) — Police in Finland said Thursday they had detained five suspects in connection with deadly violence in southeastern Nigeria and were seeking a court extension of the detentions.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: International Criminal Court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu, Hamas leader
item.description: The International Criminal Court (ICC) has issued arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Mohammed Diab Ibrahim Al-Masri, a Hamas leader commonly known as Mohammed Deif who is presumed dead, according to separate press releases.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: 19 Senators back limiting offensive weapons to Israel in key vote
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday failed to pass three resolutions that would have limited offensive arms to Israel, but 19 senators supported the initiative, showing there is growing resistance to the Israeli war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Archaeologists discover 12,000-year-old pebbles that could provide new insights about the wheel
item.description: Researchers gave a detailed look into perforated stones collected out of excavations in Israel, and came to the conclusion that they could have served as spindle whorls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: US allies accuse Russia of 'escalating hybrid activities' against NATO, EU nations after data cables severed
item.description: U.S. allies accuse Russia of "escalating hybrid activities" against NATO and EU countries after two data cables were severed in the Baltic Sea this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Australian zoo asks citizens to catch deadly spiders
item.description: Despite the zany ask, there is a valiant reason behind the call from The Australian Reptile Park in Sydney, New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation
